pr.title: Publish packages for building Melee and generating documentation
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Replaced ad-hoc CI setup for building Melee and publishing docs with reusable GitHub Container Registry packages. The PR added Docker/entrypoint/setup files for Linux builds, Windows build smoke tests, and documentation generation; split workflows into build-melee, publish-packages, and publish-pages; removed the old build.yml and publish.yml workflows; and made small Makefile adjustments for Windows detection, assembler selection, and Python invocation. The stated PR body is sparse and points to PR #751 for details, but the diff clearly shows the goal: prebuild and publish containers that encapsulate devkitPPC, WiBo, MWCC compilers, Python requirements, Doxygen, and documentation tooling so CI and PRs can build/test using those packages.
what_changed: Added a new publish-packages GitHub Actions workflow that builds container images for build-linux, build-windows, and gen-pages, tests them, tags them with docker/metadata-action, and pushes them to ghcr.io on push events.
Added a new build-melee workflow that pulls the published build-linux and build-windows images from GHCR and runs Melee builds for both GENERATE_MAP=1 and NON_MATCHING=1.
Added a new publish-pages workflow that pulls a gen-pages image, generates documentation into an output volume, uploads the generated pages artifact, and deploys to GitHub Pages on main/master.
Deleted the previous .github/workflows/build.yml, which directly installed tools, downloaded WiBo and compilers, built on Ubuntu and Windows runners, and calculated progress inline.
Deleted the previous .github/workflows/publish.yml, which directly installed Doxygen/Python dependencies and deployed pages without a reusable generator image.

Added Linux build container files that copy WiBo from ghcr.io/decompals/wibo, copy powerpc-eabi-as from devkitpro/devkitppc, download the MELEE_COMPILERS.zip bundle, expose /input and /output volumes, run make with MAKE_FLAGS, generate PROGRESS.md when GALE01.map exists, and copy build outputs.

Added Windows build container files based on mcr.microsoft.com/windows/servercore:ltsc2022. The setup uses Scoop to install git, python, and mingw, creates a Python venv, installs requirements, downloads MELEE_COMPILERS.zip, and stores compilers under LocalAppData/Melee.

Added a Windows entrypoint that clones the mounted input, activates the venv, refreshes Python requirements, copies mwcc_compiler into tools/mwcc_compiler, invokes make through MinGW bash because the Makefile does not support cmd or PowerShell, and copies build output.

Added docs generator container files that install git, doxygen, Python, a venv, requirements.txt dependencies, and doxygen-awesome-css; the entrypoint runs doxygen, creates .nojekyll, runs tools/m2ctx/m2ctx.py -pqr, and copies generated HTML to /output.
Added .dockerignore entries for .git, __pycache__, venv, and .idea to keep Docker build contexts cleaner.

Updated the Makefile to use OS=Windows_NT for Windows detection instead of checking uname for MINGW/MSYS, to select tools/mwcc_compiler/powerpc-eabi-as.exe on Windows, and to use python instead of python3.
Added empty requirements.txt placeholder files under tools/calcprogress and tools/calcprogress/src.
smart_moves: Moved expensive, fragile setup steps out of every CI build and into reusable container images, reducing repeated downloads and making build environments easier to reproduce.
Separated package publication from normal Melee building and documentation publishing, so package changes can be tested and pushed independently from build consumers.
Smoke-tested packages before publishing by running GENERATE_MAP=1, NON_MATCHING=1, and docs generation against the just-built images.
Used matrix builds for GENERATE_MAP=1 and NON_MATCHING=1 to keep matching and non-matching build modes covered.
Generated PROGRESS.md inside the Linux build package when GALE01.map exists, then surfaced it through the GitHub step summary in the build workflow.
Handled fork/PR image naming by choosing the image repository from github.repository for pushes and github.event.repository.full_name for pull_request events.

Accounted for Windows container quirks explicitly: PowerShell StrictMode/ErrorActionPreference, manual $LASTEXITCODE checks for native commands, unprivileged user setup, ACL adjustments for mounted output directories, and using MinGW bash to call make.
Used volumes /input and /output for packages, making the containers behave like reusable build/documentation tools rather than workflows tied to one checkout path.
Kept GitHub Pages deployment as a separate deploy job gated to main/master, while allowing docs generation itself to run on PRs.
decomp_lessons: For decomp projects with proprietary compiler bundles and platform-sensitive toolchains, reusable container packages can stabilize CI and developer-environment testing better than re-downloading tools in each workflow.
Keep both matching and non-matching build modes in CI matrices; changes to build scripts or toolchain paths can break one mode but not the other.
When generating maps for progress tracking, make the map-producing path also run the progress calculator and preserve the output as a CI summary/artifact.
Windows support should be tested through the same Makefile entrypoints developers use, but wrappers may be needed because Makefile shell assumptions may not hold under cmd or PowerShell.

If a container is intended as a developer-experience smoke test, document what it does and does not cover; this PR explicitly notes the Windows package is slow, installs venv/requirements without using all of them directly, and does not generate docs or PROGRESS.md.
Using a blank or minimal requirements.txt placeholder can satisfy tooling paths that expect per-tool requirements files, but future maintainers should verify whether placeholders are still needed.
naming_conventions: Workflow names were made purpose-specific and kebab-cased: build-melee, publish-packages, publish-pages.
Container package directories and image suffixes use target names matching the matrix target values: build-linux, build-windows, gen-pages.
Environment variables are uppercase and explicit: REGISTRY, IMAGE_NAME, CONTAINERFILE, MAKE_FLAGS, MELEE_COMPILERS_URL, WIBO_PATH, DEVKITPRO, DEVKITPPC.
Build flags are passed through a single MAKE_FLAGS variable containing strings such as GENERATE_MAP=1 and NON_MATCHING=1.
Mounted container interfaces use simple input/output volume names: /input and /output on Linux, C:/Input and C:/Output on Windows.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The Linux build image copies powerpc-eabi-as from devkitpro/devkitppc and exposes DEVKITPRO, DEVKITPPC, and PATH so the assembler toolchain is available consistently.
The Makefile now selects AS as tools/mwcc_compiler/powerpc-eabi-as.exe on Windows when OS=Windows_NT, while non-Windows keeps DEVKITPPC/bin/powerpc-eabi-as.
The build workflow continues to exercise GENERATE_MAP=1 and NON_MATCHING=1, preserving CI coverage for matching map generation and non-matching build paths.
The Linux entrypoint invokes make with WINE set to the copied WiBo path, supporting MWCC execution on Linux without requiring host Wine setup.

Progress calculation uses tools/calcprogress/calcprogress.py with main.dol, GALE01.map, --asm-obj-ext .s.o, and --old-map true, matching the old workflow behavior but moving it into the build container output path.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided slice.
The only review record shown was a dismissed review by r-burns with an empty body, so no reviewer rationale or requested changes can be extracted.
The PR body itself is brief and refers readers to PR #751 for details; conclusions beyond the diff are therefore low-evidence.
follow_up_queries: PR #751 package publishing context and issue-1610248156
later changes to publish-packages.yml or GHCR image naming
Melee CI build-linux build-windows container regressions
